title: Devteam trigger-driven skills — ADR-0128 §11 (WF-0064)
note: The six skills auto-apply by deterministic signal (CMIS/DAS band, profile, declared flags) — never by the user remembering a slash command. Selection is not proof: actual application is recorded via the §18 skill-application receipt. Shadow-only at WF-0064; enforcement power arrives with WF-0067 under ADR-0129 ceilings. The fail-open baseline is BASELINE_SKILLS in runtime/devteam/required-skills.mjs (deliberately hardcoded — it must survive a missing table).

senior-implementation.title: Senior implementation discipline
senior-implementation.summary: Smallest safe diff for the approved use case: respect the packet, preserve dependency direction and invariants, tests with the code, evidence with the diff.

domain-modeling.title: Domain modeling
domain-modeling.summary: Turn intent and rules into an explicit model: bounded contexts, ubiquitous language, entities/value objects, aggregates only when invariants exist, state authority, transactional boundaries.

modular-design.title: Modular design
modular-design.summary: Deliberate module boundaries and public surfaces for multi-module work: dependency direction, coupling and cycles, intentional contracts — without domain-model ceremony.

ddd-architecture-review.title: DDD architecture review
ddd-architecture-review.summary: Review domain-driven work for layer honesty: domain importing infrastructure, second state authority, contract change without a Decision, transaction crossing aggregates.

domain-test-strategy.title: Domain test strategy
domain-test-strategy.summary: Test invariants, value objects, state transitions, valid/invalid commands, events, idempotency and cross-context contracts — behavior, not implementation details.

implementation-review.title: Implementation review
implementation-review.summary: Packet-vs-diff review for non-trivial code: every changed line traces to the request, contracts honored, no abstraction or fragmentation without benefit.
